goal: Manage work-related stress day-to-day and long-term with practical strategies.
Optimize use of wellness stipend for stress reduction (therapy or gym).
Delegate more effectively to junior PMs and engineers while maintaining oversight.
constraints: Confidentiality and privacy expectations for EAP sessions; limit information shared with HR/manager.
decisions: Decide to prepare talking points and questions for HR/EAP conversations.
open: Set up daily focus block: create calendar event 60-90 minutes, mark Busy, enable DND, update status
Schedule weekly office hours with manager (60-90 minutes) and 10-15 minute priority sync
Post one-line status in team channel explaining focus time
Introduce box breathing as a default coping tool for spikes
Begin using 2-minute rule focusing on the Most Important Task (MIT); log progress
Add One-breath reset as a default micro-habit; trigger: before opening email or meeting
Add 4-4 box breath as a trigger-based micro-habit
Add 10-second body scan before Slack/DM
Set up Two-minute MIT first-step task with timer
Create One-sentence plan at laptop start
Create Priority triage quick reply template
Configure Mini-DND toggle (30-60 minutes) with one keystroke
Add Delay reply signature template
Implement Drink-and-move at 30s stretch and water break
Add 60s walk break to routine
Habit-stacking trigger: attach to computer login
Place Visual anchor sticky note 'Breathe' or '1-MIT' on laptop lid
Implement 1-week trial plan: morning ritual, midday reset, and evening unwind; track daily completion and variations.
Add or define evening unwind steps (brain dump, highlight, tomorrow's plan, light physical unwind) within daily routine.
Test midday reset micro-variations: Mon gratitude note, Tue phone-free walk, Wed social check-in, Thu mindful breathing, Fri movement flow; log outcomes.
Protect a real lunch break by calendar blocking 12:00–1:00 daily; mark Busy and enable DND.
Create and share boundary scripts for lunch with team and manager (offline 12:00–1:00, async responses).
Identify restorative lunch break options (e.g., 15–30 min walk, 10 min mindful breathing, offline hobby) and schedule at least one this week.

Implement Slack notification reductions: adjust preferences (Direct messages, mentions & keywords), schedule Do Not Disturb during focus blocks, mute noisy channels, use keywords with a prefix for urgent items, and post a brief team notice outlining policy.
Initiate delegation framework: adopt one-message delegation template, define checkpoints and escalation triggers; delegate one task this week and report outcomes.
Identify low-risk tasks suitable for delegation and assign to junior PMs/engineers; align with growth goals.

Try one of the 10-minute morning routines and log outcomes; pick calmly depending on desired tone (calm, energy, meeting-readiness) and integrate a fallback (one-breath + one-sentence plan) if interrupted.
Create standard weekly 1:1 agenda template (30-minute and 15-minute versions) with prep requirements and timeboxing.
Add one-line pre-read requirement to 1:1 agenda and shared doc.
Draft one-sentence email/slack to announce the new 1:1 agenda to reports.
Draft HR and EAP conversation prep: goals, impact examples, possible accommodations, and questions.
Create copy/paste scripts for EAP and HR conversations.
Clarify confidentiality and record-keeping with HR/EAP (who can access notes, how stored).
Add 2-5 minute micro-breaks to daily schedule; pilot for 2 weeks, tied to end-of-meeting transitions
